howItWorks.intro: This wire ampacity calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Derated Ampacity
rules.explanation: Standard ampacity reduced for high ambient temperatures.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What wire size for 20 amps?
faq.answer: 12 AWG minimum for 20-amp circuits per NEC code.

faq.question: Does temperature affect wire capacity?
faq.answer: Yes, high ambient temperatures reduce ampacity by 12-25%.

faq.question: Is the Wire Ampacity Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What wire gauge should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate wire gauge value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Wire Ampacity Calculator?
sections.body: The Wire Ampacity Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your wire gauge, wire type, ambient temperature, you get instant results including maximum ampacity, recommended breaker, typical use. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting maximum ampacity right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your wire gauge and need to find the right maximum ampacity. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Wire Gauge (AWG)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your wire gauge in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Wire Type
howToSteps.description: Type or select your wire type in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Ambient Temperature
howToSteps.description: Type or select your ambient temperature in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your maximum ampacity, recommended breaker, typical use. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your wire gauge before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both maximum ampacity and recommended breaker — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your ambient temperature, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
